The Axon backend contract requires a generated GraphQL client plus a WebSocket subscription client, but the current implementation is an in-process JSONL emulation instead of a typed Axon package. The gap is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-26, which says the backend is a local emulation and not the wire-path implementation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 explicitly keeps the backend as a JSONL-shaped emulation, so there is nowhere for genqlient-generated bindings, schema snapshots, query documents, or a subscription transport to live. The repo has no Axon client package to satisfy TD-030's GraphQL-only requirement.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql with the pinned introspection snapshot.
- Add query/mutation/subscription documents under cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/.
- Add genqlient config and generated Go bindings under cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Add a small subscription transport in the same package for GraphQL WS changeEvents.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing backend_axon.go itself; that is the follow-up implementation bead.
- Config routing and package-level test matrix changes.

46:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker exec's the resolved ddx binary (os.Executable() with DDX_EXECUTABLE override) with subcommand 'work' and a flag-vector derived from ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. No in-process call to worker.Run remains in this code path.
2. WorkerRecord.PID is populated with the subprocess PID before the exec returns to StartExecuteLoop; signal-based Stop and watchdog reaping continue to work against the child PID (TestStopExecuteLoopWorker_KillsSubprocess covers SIGTERM→SIGKILL grace).
3. Progress events from the child reach the parent's drainProgress goroutine via the existing /api/workers/<id>/event ingestion path (server.go:884). At least one new test TestRunWorker_ProgressBridge_FromChild asserts that an event POSTed by the child appears on a subscribed SSE channel.
4. BeadWorkerFactory is deleted from WorkerManager. workers_test.go/workers_e2e_test.go/workers_stop_propagation_test.go/workers_route_preflight_test.go/workers_config_test.go/graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go updated: tests that depended on injecting an in-process executor are rewritten using the test-reentry pattern (ddx-work-stub) or deleted with rationale comment if the semantics moved into cli/cmd/* tests.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-03T22:59:39.685160971Z","description":"PROBLEM: cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker runs the bead-execution loop in-process, knitting together LandCoordinator, DefaultBeadReviewer, CostCapTracker, RoutePreflight, ExecuteBeadWorker, ProgressCh→drainProgress, WakeCh, BeadWorkerFactory injection, and watchdog phase-stall tracking — all of which contradict ADR-022's autonomous-default model where workers are subprocesses identical to operator-launched 'ddx work'.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-940 (runWorker) builds an in-process executor closure (singleTierAttempt at lines 679-747), wraps it with a CostCap tracker (lines 753-797), wires a DefaultBeadReviewer (lines 801-809), and calls worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{...}). All of this duplicates work that 'ddx work' (cli/cmd/work.go → cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:runAgentExecuteLoopImpl) already does, just inside the parent server process.

PROPOSED FIX: Replace the in-process worker.Run(...) call with exec.CommandContext(ctx, dddxBin, 'work', <flags>) where dddxBin = os.Executable() (override-able via DDX_EXECUTABLE env for tests). Project root passed via --project flag or DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env. Provider/harness/model/profile/effort/poll-interval/once/no-review/review-harness/review-model/label-filter/model-ref translated to existing ddx work flags. Capture child PID into WorkerRecord.PID (already a field at workers.go:147). Stop() and the watchdog already signal via PID (workers.go:1070, 1279) — verify the cross-process path works; SIGTERM with kill grace then SIGKILL.

Progress event ingestion changes: child process emits via workerprobe to /api/workers/<id>/event (this endpoint exists, server.go:884). The parent's WorkerManager subscribes to those events (in-memory derived view from the workers ingestion handler) and forwards into the per-subprocess workerHandle.progressCh so existing SSE subscribers and drainProgress logic continue to work. Mapping: parent records the WorkerRecord.id at exec time; child registers with /api/workers/register and gets a probe-issued worker_id; parent correlates by ProjectRoot + ExecutorPID + StartedAt window.

Drop in-process LandCoordinator/Reviewer/CostCap/RoutePreflight wiring inside runWorker (they now run inside the child). LandCoordinators registry stays for the existing inline 'ddx work' path (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1714).

WakeCh: replace with SIGUSR1 to the child (or document as no-op for the v1 of this refactor; WakeProject still walks workers but only sends to live in-process handles — kept null-safe for subprocess workers).

BeadWorkerFactory: removed. Tests that previously injected a fake executor migrate to either (a) reentry-stub mode where the test binary acts as 'ddx work' via go test re-exec pattern, or (b) deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now live in cli/cmd/* tests).

NON-SCOPE: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker rewrite (covered by 7b). Deletion of ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct (covered by 7b). End-to-end real-claude integration (covered by 7c). UI/GraphQL surface changes (already covered by ADR-022 step 5).","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T225413-91120fa0-5be5f460","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:server","kind:refactor","adr:022"],"parent":"ddx-eccc6efb","priority":2,"status":"closed","title":"ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker exec's ddx work subprocess (parent-side plumbing)","updated_at":"2026-05-03T23:00:18.503933642Z"}

## Work

Capture real-world failure artifacts from the review-malfunction-report into cli/internal/agent/testdata/ (or equivalent location next to the reviewer code):

Update FEAT-006 (or wherever the execute-loop outcome contract is documented) to list the new structured outcomes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"ee729c4aed85c64e3f19a5f76d2addabd815918b","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:16:41.722078707Z","description":"When ddx agent execute-bead lands a commit locally but merging into the base branch produces conflicts, the loop today preserves the result at refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<ts>-<base> and unclaims the bead with status: land_conflict. The bead reopens; subsequent attempts re-execute from scratch — burning more agent time and dollars even though the original work is intact in the preserved ref.

OBSERVED 2026-04-29 on ddx-52469547 (FEAT-015 bead 3, 'runtime fallback removal + audit-side migration'):
- Sonnet ran for ~3h, produced commit 12fbf6dd1b98 covering all 7 AC items.
- The base branch advanced significantly during the run (V1, V2, V3 vision beads + dogfood + concurrent work).
- The run hit the agent's 3h hard timeout right at the test phase.
- execute-loop preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-52469547/20260429T175646Z-aa7b31785a44 with status execution_failed (timeout, but functionally identical to land_conflict for the merge story).
- Operator attempted git merge --no-ff 12fbf6dd, hit 7 file-level conflicts (mechanical drift from parallel beads), aborted, and only avoided wasting the work because a parallel attempt (263b5eff) had landed the exact same scope cleanly. That parallel landing is a coincidence, not a guarantee.

The general case: a preserved iteration represents real money spent and real work done. land_conflict / execution_failed-with-result that bails to 'reopen and re-run' throws that work away. The loop should at least attempt to reuse the preserved result.

REQUESTED BEHAVIOR

1. On land_conflict (merge produces conflicts when applying the iteration onto current tip), execute-loop should:
   a. Try a 3-way merge of the preserved iteration ref onto current main using merge.conflictStyle=diff3 and ort strategy with -X ours/-X theirs heuristics. Only if the merge resolves cleanly under those heuristics, commit it as a non-fast-forward merge with a message like 'Merge preserved iteration ddx-<id> after base drift'.
   b. If 3-way auto-resolution fails, dispatch a SMALL focused agent run (cheap tier) with the conflict files, the preserved iteration's commit message, and the bead AC. Its sole job: resolve the conflicts, verify build/tests, commit. Do NOT re-do the bead from scratch.
   c. Only if (a) and (b) both fail, fall back to today's behavior (preserve, unclaim, set short cooldown, mark for human review with a structured outcome distinct from generic execution_failed — perhaps kind:land-conflict-unresolvable).

2. Cooldown for land_conflict-unresolvable should be short (5-30 min), not the 24h cap, since the conflict will resolve faster as soon as a human or another bead lands a clarifying change.

3. Same logic should apply to execution_failed-with-preserved-result (timeouts mid-merge): if a commit was produced before the failure, attempt to merge it before discarding the run.

4. Reuse the auto-recover plumbing landed for push race in ddx-a458af7c (landPushAutoRecover, capLoopCooldown). Specifically the conflict-vs-clean-recovery branching should mirror landPushAutoRecover's structure.

5. Regression tests:
   - Fake git that produces a mechanical conflict resolvable via -X ours: assert auto-resolution succeeds and bead lands.
   - Fake git that produces an irreconcilable structural conflict: assert escalation to focused-resolve agent (or, at minimum, that today's behavior is taken with the new structured outcome).
   - Preserved-iteration-on-timeout case: assert the preserved commit IS attempted as a merge before falling back.

ACCEPTANCE OF FAILURE: if the focused-resolve agent itself fails or escalates to BLOCKING, that's a structured outcome (kind:land-conflict-needs-human) — the bead is parked with the conflict context and preserved ref clearly recorded.

Related: ddx-a458af7c (push race auto-recover, this session's predecessor work).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"sonnet","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:47:14.013496692Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Diff at rev 1550c5382a is a bare merge commit with no code changes; none of the 8 AC items (3-way auto-resolve, focused-resolve agent dispatch, structured outcomes, cooldown cap, timeout-with-preserved-commit path, regression tests, CHANGELOG, FEAT-006 update) can be evaluated as implemented.

  `ddx --version` exits with an unknown-flag error even though `ddx version` works. Operators commonly guess top-level `--version` for modern CLIs; DDx should support both forms so discovery works without removing or changing the existing `version` subcommand.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/cmd/command_factory.go:124-127 registers root persistent flags for `--config`, `--verbose`/`-v`, and `--library-base-path`, but no root `--version` flag exists. cli/cmd/command_factory.go:450-466 defines the `version` subcommand and prints the desired version/commit/build output there only. cli/cmd/command_factory.go:130-147 runs root PersistentPreRunE for normal command execution; a root `--version` path must print version info and exit successfully before ordinary command validation/update checks make the flag behave like a missing command.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add a root persistent `--version` boolean flag in cli/cmd/command_factory.go near the other root flags.
  - Factor the existing version-printing body from the `version` subcommand into a shared helper so `ddx version` and `ddx --version` return the same concise DDx version, commit, and build output.
  - Wire root flag handling so `ddx --version` exits 0, writes to command stdout, does not require a subcommand, and does not consume `-v` because `-v` remains the verbose flag.
  - Add focused tests in cli/cmd/version_acceptance_test.go for `TestRootVersionFlag_PrintsVersionAndBuildInfo` and `TestVersionCommand_StillPrintsVersionAndBuildInfo` (or update the existing acceptance test with those exact subtests).

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not remove or rename `ddx version`.
  - Do not change `-v`; it remains verbose output.
  - Do not alter `ddx status`, plugin version checks, installation/update behavior, or release metadata formatting beyond sharing the existing version output helper.

PARENT
  This is a root-level CLI ergonomics bug; no suitable open CLI umbrella bead exists.

DEPS
  No deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-00a8ed79/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-00a8ed79","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:2","area:cli","kind:fix","ux","discovery"],"priority":1,"session_id":"eb-472ec4bd","status":"closed","title":"cli: support root --version flag","updated_at":"2026-05-06T03:12:13.130274954Z"}

3. Non-semver inputs still tiebreak by shortest-suffix: fuzzy('gpt', ['gpt-4o', 'gpt-4-turbo']) returns gpt-4o (shorter).

At least 6 table-driven tests in discovery_test.go covering semver + non-semver + mixed cases.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"5328120be55ab6af07a5cd35c7dc7f7a22f30f96","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:14:30.806468726Z","description":"## Problem (filed per opus fresh-eyes review 2026-04-17)

After ddx-0486e601 lands normalization, fuzzy matcher still has an inherited tiebreak quirk:

    Input: 'qwen3'
    Canonicalized pool: ['qwen3.5-27b-4bit', 'qwen3.6-35b-a3b-4bit']
    Current tiebreak: shortest-suffix wins -> picks qwen3.5 (shorter suffix '.5-27b-4bit' < '.6-35b-a3b-4bit')

A user asking for 'qwen3' almost certainly wants the LATEST, not whichever happens to have a shorter concrete suffix. Shortest-suffix is a reasonable default for avoiding ambiguity, but for semver-like suffixes it produces counterintuitive results.

Opus: 'Not a blocker — inherited behavior — but add a test asserting the current (possibly-wrong) resolution and flag it for a follow-up. Making this a conscious policy choice for a future change.'

## Fix

Add a semver-aware tiebreak when multiple candidates share a prefix and the first character after the prefix is a digit (indicating a version suffix). Otherwise fall back to shortest-suffix.

Heuristic:

1. Group candidates by the normalized prefix+first-version-segment (e.g. 'qwen3.5' vs 'qwen3.6').
2. Within each group, pick by shortest-suffix (current rule).
3. Across groups, pick the HIGHEST version by semver-like comparison.

Refuse to over-engineer: no full semver parsing, just 'compare version segments as numbers where possible, fall back to lexicographic'.

Corner cases to test:
- 'qwen3' -> qwen3.6-35b-a3b-4bit (prefer qwen3.6 over qwen3.5)
- 'qwen3.5' -> qwen3.5-27b-4bit (exact prefix match, only one candidate)
- 'gpt-4' -> gpt-4o (if present) vs gpt-4-turbo — semver-free, fall back to shortest-suffix (current behavior)
- 'minimax-m' -> prefer minimax-m2.7 over minimax-m2.5

## Files touched

- cli/internal/agent/discovery.go — new tiebreak in fuzzyMatchInPool
- cli/internal/agent/discovery_test.go — semver + non-semver tiebreak cases

60:{"acceptance":"New file cli/internal/agent/script.go implements RunScript(opts) that reads a directive file, executes directives against opts.WorkDir using real filesystem and git operations, and returns a real Result. The harness is registered in cli/internal/agent/registry.go as `script` and marked always-available in Discover(). Directive grammar supports at minimum: append-line, create-file, delete-file, commit, set-exit, no-op, sleep-ms, fail-during. Environment variable interpolation supports ${DDX_BEAD_ID}, ${DDX_ATTEMPT_ID}, ${DDX_WORKER_ID}. Unit tests in cli/internal/agent/script_test.go cover: (1) append-line + commit produces a real commit with the expected content, (2) no-op produces zero commits and exit 0, (3) create-file + commit produces the file and a commit, (4) set-exit 1 makes the run return exit 1, (5) fail-during 2 executes directives 0 and 1 but errors on directive 2, (6) env var interpolation replaces ${DDX_BEAD_ID} with the value from Correlation, (7) invoking via `ddx agent run --harness=script --model=<file>` works end-to-end with a real temp git repo. go test ./internal/agent -run TestScript completes in under 5 seconds.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:49:12.379615428Z","description":"The execute-loop / execute-bead / land-coordinator system cannot be tested end-to-end today because every "fake" agent in the codebase returns a canned Result{} without actually writing files or making commits. `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go` is a prompt-response dictionary replayer — it returns canned stdout based on prompt hash but does not touch the filesystem or git. `fakeAgentRunner` and `fakeExecuteBeadGit` in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go` are in-memory mocks that track expected call counts. Neither produces actual commits that the orchestration layer can rebase, merge, or land.

The consequence: every test that claims to cover concurrency, serialization, or git orchestration uses mocked git and therefore cannot catch merge-race, rebase-conflict, or coordinator-serialization bugs. `TestConcurrentWorkersNoMergeRace` at execute_bead_orchestrator_test.go:228 is particularly misleading — it uses separate orchTestGitOps instances per worker so the shared-writer invariant is never exercised. The test passes because no shared state exists to race on.

## What this bead builds

A new deterministic `script` harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go, mirroring cli/internal/agent/virtual.go):

- Registered alongside claude/codex/agent/virtual in cli/internal/agent/registry.go
- Always available (no binary probe required)
- Takes a directive file (passed via RunOptions.Model or a new RunOptions.ScriptFile field — either works)
- Reads the directive file, executes each line against opts.WorkDir as real filesystem + git operations
- Returns a real Result{ExitCode, Output} that the loop and orchestrator can observe

## Directive grammar

Line-based, comments start with `#`. Each directive performs a real side effect:

```
append-line <path> <text>          # append text to file (create if missing)
create-file <path> <content>       # create or overwrite file
delete-file <path>                 # rm -f path
modify-line <path> <regex> <repl>  # sed-style in-place substitution
run <shell-command>                # exec in WorkDir with clean env
commit <message>                   # git add -A && git commit -m <message>
set-exit <code>                    # final exit code (default 0)
no-op                              # no commits, no files, exit 0
fail-during <index>                # synthetic failure at the Nth directive
sleep-ms <n>                       # deterministic delay (for scheduling tests)
```

Directives run in order. Environment variables DDX_BEAD_ID, DDX_ATTEMPT_ID, DDX_WORKER_ID are interpolated into text arguments so directives can be parameterized. Example:

```
append-line tests/smoke.txt "hello from ${DDX_BEAD_ID}"
commit "smoke: ${DDX_BEAD_ID}"
```

## Invocation

From the CLI: `ddx agent run --harness=script --model=./testdata/append-smoke.txt --prompt=/dev/null`

Programmatically from a test:
```go
runner := agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{Harness: "script"})
result, err := runner.Run(agent.RunOptions{
    WorkDir: wtPath,
    Model:   "testdata/append-smoke.txt",
    Correlation: map[string]string{"bead_id": beadID, "attempt_id": attemptID},
})
```

## Implementation notes

- Mirror the dispatch pattern in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:81 where `virtual` is intercepted
- Always-available in Discover() same as virtual + agent
- No external dependencies beyond what the agent package already uses (os, os/exec for git)
- Estimated size: ~200 lines of implementation + ~200 lines of tests
- Unit tests in cli/internal/agent/script_test.go must prove each directive actually performs its claimed side effect on a real temp git repo

## What this enables

Every test case in beads B (integration tests) and C (server runWorker regression test) depends on this harness existing. Without it, the land-coordinator redesign ships behind whatever real claude happens to do on the day the test runs.","id":"ddx-025046ac","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:testing","kind:infrastructure","phase:build"],"parent":"ddx-8746d8a6","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-53f5df0","status":"closed","title":"Implement deterministic script harness for orchestration stress testing","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:00:05.850093133Z"}

64:{"acceptance":"1) TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-24T04:36:17Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"6effebaea570aae432cb01e7d4456d56132e045c","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:42.174990312Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-02e5e3ba","depends_on_id":"ddx-915240dd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:42:24Z"}],"description":"Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. 1298:{"acceptance":"Given a local plugin manifest sets install.root.target under `~/`, when `ddx install --local` runs inside a project, then both the global installed root and `.ddx/plugins/<name>` exist and project-relative plugin resource paths resolve","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T04:31:27Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"569068","closing_commit_sha":"8e1727512319acb8b916ebbebbccb7607924a9c7","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:21:30.705389801Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/install.go:244
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Registry installs explicitly create `.ddx/plugins/<name>` as a project-local symlink when the manifest root target is global (`~/...`) so project-relative plugin paths still resolve. The new manifest-driven local install path does not mirror that behavior: it creates the global symlink only. Repro: a local plugin whose manifest sets `install.root.target: ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin` produces `global_plugin_exists=yes`, `project_plugin_exists=no`, and still installs skills. Any code or docs that resolve plugin resources through `.ddx/plugins/<name>/...` now break in local developer installs.
Suggested fix: When `installLocal` honors a global root target, also create the same project-local `.ddx/plugins/<name>` symlink that `InstallPackage` creates for registry installs.","id":"hx-3f3c0e74","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: keep project-local plugin path for local installs with global roots","updated_at":"2026-04-10T04:33:09.938605658Z"}

1309:{"acceptance":"SD-015 defines a stable model-level attribution key for routing metrics so RoutingOutcome, QuotaSnapshot, and any derived burn/performance summaries can be associated with the resolved candidate model or canonical target; FEAT-014 stays consistent with that key; and the docs no longer imply that latency/cost/reliability from different models on the same surface are mixed into one routing signal","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T03:46:32Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"327749","closing_commit_sha":"0364b777bb79faf9eeef12a4d940b190d687c894","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:45:04.557331352Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new minimal-routing schema records `RoutingOutcome`, `QuotaSnapshot`, and `BurnSummary` only by `harness` and `surface`. Earlier in the same design, however, each `CandidatePlan` carries `CanonicalTarget`, `ConcreteModel`, `EstimatedCostUSD`, and `PerformanceMetrics` because routing decisions can differ materially by resolved model or exact pin. A single surface can host very different models with different latency, reliability, and cost characteristics. If DDx aggregates outcome and burn data only at the harness/surface level, runs for one model will contaminate routing signals for another, making model-specific scoring and exact-pin behavior incorrect.
Suggested fix: Extend the minimal routing-metrics schema with a model-identifying dimension such as `canonical_target` or `concrete_model` (or explicitly define an equivalent normalization key), and update FEAT-014/SD-015 so performance, cost, and burn inputs remain attributable to the resolved model rather than only the surface.","id":"hx-4a12a619","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: keep routing metrics model-specific","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:47:24.607997607Z"}

File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:1
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.
Suggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance model that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.","id":"hx-63ac1232","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit","updated_at":"2026-04-09T23:07:24.624928924Z"}

In a temp bead store, `go run . bead evidence add <id> ...` followed by `go run . bead update <id> --unset events` makes `ddx bead evidence list <id> --json` return `[]`, so this patch gives normal metadata updates a data-loss path that can erase audit evidence and violate the append-only contract of `ddx bead evidence`.

1350:{"acceptance":"SD-016 states that cost_usd=-1 means unknown rather than known cost, direct cost aggregation excludes the sentinel value, and the cost_state / cost precedence rules align with FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 sentinel semantics","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T23:00:02Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2091135","created_at":"2026-04-09T22:35:00.80036448Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md:191
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: SD-016 defines `cost_state=known` whenever a session row reports `cost_usd` and says bead lifecycle cost should use `session.cost_usd` whenever it is present. But FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 explicitly define `cost_usd=-1` as the sentinel for unknown cost, not a real price. Implementing SD-016 as written would treat unknown-cost sessions as known values, producing negative dollar totals and skipping the estimation/unknown fallback for a core FEAT-016 metric.
Suggested fix: Update SD-016 so `cost_usd=-1` is mapped to `cost_state=unknown` and excluded from direct cost sums, with estimation or unknown fallback applied only to non-sentinel values.","id":"hx-72b9a368","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: treat cost_usd=-1 as unknown in SD-016","updated_at":"2026-04-10T00:21:23.11757511Z"}

1352:{"acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-010a50cf --json omits closing_commit_sha, and closing a metadata-only review bead through the supported ddx CLI no longer stamps the prior HEAD or unrelated metadata commit as closing provenance when no new close commit lands","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T10:22:57Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2123793","created_at":"2026-04-10T09:37:30.081467159Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:27
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: During this last-commit review, the supported `ddx bead close hx-010a50cf` flow closed a metadata-only `action:review` bead and persisted `closing_commit_sha=d20a24fd8ff635481f19c934e8643472ed0a7cde` on the new tracker row. That SHA is the previously reviewed provenance-repair commit, not a close commit created by the review workflow, and FEAT-019 explicitly says tracker-only backfills should omit `closing_commit_sha` rather than point replay at an unrelated metadata or earlier implementation boundary. The repo source and tests still indicate review beads should suppress this field, so the durable tracker state produced by the current supported CLI path remains misleading.
Suggested fix: Make the supported bead-close path omit `closing_commit_sha` for metadata-only review closes when no landed close commit exists, add regression coverage for that no-new-commit path, and repair hx-010a50cf so its closing provenance is absent rather than pointing at d20a24f.","id":"hx-73a3c3c0","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: metadata-only review close still stamps prior HEAD provenance","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:25:57.586690085Z"}
1353:{"acceptance":"With git.auto_commit=always in a git repo and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' clean, and 'git show HEAD -- .ddx/beads.jsonl' includes the bead row with closing_commit_sha equal to 'git rev-parse HEAD' rather than requiring an extra uncommitted worktree update","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T23:35:05Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2688734","closing_commit_sha":"c42a407f810629b99954f1258496f66e8d060635","created_at":"2026-04-09T23:33:20.048985185Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:518
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new close flow writes bead status first, auto-commits .ddx/beads.jsonl, and only then writes closing_commit_sha back to the store. In a clean temp git repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' dirty with ' M .ddx/beads.jsonl'. The landed HEAD commit 9494afc82408cac9a650d616fc3207197e1dfbd6 contains only the status=open->closed change, while the durable closing_commit_sha appears only in the uncommitted worktree diff. That means the command still fails to land close provenance atomically and leaves replay/audit metadata outside the recorded close commit.
Suggested fix: Restructure bead close so the recorded closing_commit_sha is present in the same landed commit that closes the bead, or perform and persist a second deterministic commit/update so git status is clean after close and HEAD contains the final provenance-bearing row.","id":"hx-73c95bab","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-df089a3d","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: persist close provenance in the landed tracker commit","updated_at":"2026-04-09T23:42:54.706314959Z"}

File: cli/cmd/doctor.go:452
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: `checkInstalledPlugins` iterates every row in installed state without filtering by package type, even though `InstalledEntry` records resource installs as well as plugins. A minimal repro with an installed `resource` entry (`persona/example`) makes `ddx doctor --plugins` emit `/tmp/.../persona.md/package.yaml: open ...: not a directory` and `missing package.yaml` for a non-plugin file. This breaks the plugin audit for users who have installed single resources.
Suggested fix: Restrict `ddx doctor --plugins` to package types that actually represent plugin roots (at least `plugin`, and `workflow` if workflows are intentionally audited), and skip `resource` entries entirely.","id":"hx-74a3aa15","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/doctor.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: limit doctor --plugins to plugin installs","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:53:37.986281397Z"}

Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The new frontend-e2e job sets up Node, keys its cache from `cli/internal/server/frontend/package-lock.json`, and runs `npm ci`, `npx playwright install`, and `npm run test:e2e`. The active `typescript-bun` concern and TP-002 declare Bun as the frontend package manager and script runner, with `bun.lock` as the authoritative lockfile and `bunx`/`bun run` as the expected commands. Wiring this job to npm cements a second dependency-resolution path in CI that can drift from the declared Bun toolchain and from local/frontend build behavior.
Suggested fix: Switch the job to `oven-sh/setup-bun`, cache/install from `cli/internal/server/frontend/bun.lock`, and run the Playwright install/tests with `bunx` and `bun run` so CI matches the active concern contract.","id":"hx-76c04de5","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".github/workflows/ci.yml","status":"closed","title":"drift: run embedded frontend CI with Bun practices","updated_at":"2026-04-10T13:16:54.193462894Z"}

1367:{"acceptance":"A regression test covers a mixed log directory where one harness has routing-outcomes.jsonl rows and another harness still has post-cutoff legacy-only sessions.jsonl rows, and ddx agent usage reports both harnesses without double-counting mirrored sessions.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T10:33:43Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2175997","closing_commit_sha":"f645daf608fe4cfd02c5fbc4d038cfb687811b2f","created_at":"2026-04-10T09:50:43.170545532Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:247
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new compatibility merge uses one global `migrationCutoff` taken from the earliest routing outcome in the log directory and then drops every legacy session row at or after that timestamp before merging (`aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics` + `aggregateUsageAggs`). That still under-reports mixed stores when routing metrics exist for one harness but another harness still has legacy-only rows after that cutoff. Repro with the current binary: create `routing-outcomes.jsonl` containing only a claude row at `2026-04-09T10:00:00Z`, create `sessions.jsonl` containing that mirrored claude row plus a codex legacy row at `2026-04-10T10:00:00Z`, then run `ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01 --format json`; the output only includes claude and silently drops codex even though the log directory still contains a valid post-cutoff legacy session.
Suggested fix: Make the compatibility boundary and dedupe key harness-scoped or row-scoped rather than global, so legacy rows without a matching routing outcome remain visible until that harness/session has actually migrated; add regression coverage for a mixed directory where one harness has routing outcomes and another still only has legacy sessions after the first outcome timestamp.","id":"hx-80c953b2","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/agent_usage.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: keep legacy-only harness rows visible during mixed usage migration","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:35:14.704961333Z"}

1374:{"acceptance":"Given an installed plugin root contains an invalid package.yaml, when `ddx doctor --plugins` runs, then it reports the manifest validation error without also reporting `missing package.yaml`","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T03:23:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"194383","closing_commit_sha":"cb542de8d25c4c84f2afac39e76a7aa7628f1280","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:20:55.590477463Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/registry/audit.go:24
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `AuditInstalledEntry` unconditionally adds `missing package.yaml` whenever `loadPackageDefinitionForAudit` returns `manifestPresent=false`. For malformed manifests, `loadPackageDefinitionForAudit` also returns `false`, so doctor reports both the real validation problem and a bogus missing-file error. Repro: a plugin root with `api_version: [not-a-scalar]` produces both `unsupported api_version "[not-a-scalar]"` and `missing package.yaml`.

File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:6
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit fb9bdf1 closes ddx-7e88c022 with closing_commit_sha=4563fa7, an abbreviated SHA. The close store documents this field as the exact closing commit SHA when known, but manual close evidence is persisted verbatim. Replay later resolves closing_commit_sha through git rev-parse to choose the baseline commit, so storing a short SHA weakens audit and replay determinism as the repository grows and abbreviations can become ambiguous or invalid. In the current tracker, this reviewed row is one of only two abbreviated closing_commit_sha values; the durable convention elsewhere is full 40-character SHAs.
Suggested fix: Normalize ddx bead close --commit input through git rev-parse before storing it, repair ddx-7e88c022 to the full 40-character 4563fa77d88cfb0f242925a927e913841b43868c value, and add regression coverage for manual close provenance normalization.","id":"hx-83ed9550","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: normalize closing_commit_sha to full git SHAs","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:54:27.646433594Z"}

1389:{"acceptance":"A review bead that already carries a valid closing_commit_sha keeps that SHA after ddx bead close when no new close commit lands, while stale prior-HEAD tracker provenance is still removed for metadata-only review closes","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T10:50:03Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2270767","closing_commit_sha":"d51f18c93d88901d833c9cc82ab280611e7690d2","created_at":"2026-04-10T10:32:22.111979389Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:649
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f645daf fixes the stale-review-provenance case by deleting any existing `closing_commit_sha` before a review bead is closed without `--commit`, but the new branch cannot distinguish stale provenance from a valid implementation boundary that was intentionally set earlier. Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.
Suggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.","id":"hx-939516c7","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:51:40.697890368Z"}

File: cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:169
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The migration switched the embedded runtime to github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent, but the default preset in AgentRunConfig still uses `"forge"`, and the checked-in sample config still sets `.ddx/config.yaml` to `preset: forge`. In v0.1.0, the agent library only exposes presets `agent`, `benchmark`, `minimal`, `claude`, `codex`, and `cursor`; unknown names silently fall back to `agent`. That means DDx now ignores the configured/default preset name with no warning, which makes prompt behavior implicit and leaves the runtime/config surface out of sync with the migrated library.
Suggested fix: Update the embedded default and checked-in config to a supported preset name (`agent` or another intentional choice), remove stale forge preset references from the config surface, and add a regression test that the configured default preset is one of the embedded library's supported presets rather than relying on unknown-name fallback.","id":"hx-96df1675","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: replace unsupported forge preset defaults after ddx-agent migration","updated_at":"2026-04-10T00:33:53.097249638Z"}

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:80
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 71f97be suppresses closing_commit_sha whenever the landed close commit touches only paths under .ddx/. That is too broad: .ddx contains real governed artifacts such as config and other tracked workspace files, not just tracker backfills. In a temp repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, creating a bead, modifying a tracked .ddx/notes.txt file, and running ddx bead close lands a close commit that changes .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/notes.txt, but the bead still omits closing_commit_sha. FEAT-019 only says to omit metadata-only tracker backfills; dropping provenance for legitimate .ddx-scoped work makes replay/audit fall back to sessionless mode even though the implementation boundary is available.
Suggested fix: Detect metadata-only close commits more narrowly, for example by distinguishing pure .ddx/beads.jsonl backfills from other tracked .ddx artifacts, and add regression coverage for a close commit that lands tracked .ddx work.","id":"hx-98fec407","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve closing provenance for legitimate .ddx-only work","updated_at":"2026-04-10T00:48:20.400206217Z"}

File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:59
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: API-001 chooses a candidate using the generic execution-ready validator before it resolves the base revision and governing execution-contract snapshot. FEAT-006 says execution documents are resolved from the base revision before the agent runs, and those pre-run versions govern the current iteration. Description: Commit 5349a98 removes the per-harness cutoff and now suppresses legacy sessions.jsonl rows only when usageMirrorKey finds a matching native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id (aggregateUsageAggs lines 201-207 and aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics lines 246-270). File: cli/cmd/bead.go:567
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 1bc26181bae1e88cd6c29fc11f20c5904ed2ae8a normalizes `ddx bead close --commit` through `git rev-parse` before persisting the value. That fixes short SHAs inside a git repo, but it also makes manual close provenance unusable in non-git workspaces where bead commands otherwise still work. Reproduced with the built CLI in a temp directory outside git: `ddx bead create ...` succeeds, then `ddx bead close <id> --commit 1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678` now fails with `git rev-parse ... exit status 128`. Before this change, the command accepted an exact commit SHA verbatim. That is a behavior regression for portable/manual tracker workflows and contradicts the command contract that `--commit` records the exact closing commit SHA when known.

Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 58d6229309445ce3e571b85e14f1ba423b2198b1 still treats an explicit session row with cost_usd=0 as estimated whenever the model has non-zero pricing. Both sessionCostState and sessionDerivedCost continue to special-case only sess.CostUSD > 0, so a priced-model row such as model=gpt-5.4 with cost_usd=0 falls through to sessionEstimatedCost and is reported as cost_state=estimated with a non-zero derived cost instead of preserving the recorded zero. The new regression test only covers qwen/qwen3-coder-30b, whose estimator also returns 0, so it misses the case that actually matters for explicit zero-cost rows on priced models.

Pass 1 correctness: API-001 duplicates post-run and land/preserve ownership around Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log

Usage:
  ddx agent [flags]
  ddx agent [command]

Available Commands:
  benchmark    Run a benchmark suite comparing agent harnesses
  capabilities Show agent model and reasoning-level capabilities
  condense     Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines
  doctor       Check agent harness health
  list         List available agent harnesses
  log          Show agent session history
  replay       Replay a bead with a different agent harness or model
  run          Invoke an agent with a prompt
  usage        Show per-harness token and cost summary

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for agent

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx agent [command] --help" for more information about a command., and it validates candidate readiness before resolving the governing base snapshot that FEAT-006 says must control execution-document evaluation.

Pass 4 operational learnings: no AGENTS.md change required; no learnings bead required.

1425:{"acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-01c0012d --json omits closing_commit_sha after repair, and regression coverage proves metadata-only  flows do not stamp unrelated HEAD provenance when no landed close commit exists","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T06:01:49Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1151868","closing_commit_sha":"996ee762b4be91691d04441154c25e4696f44755","created_at":"2026-04-10T01:44:37.355670823Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:22
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: During this review, `ddx bead close hx-01c0012d` closed a metadata-only review bead without creating a new git commit, yet the resulting tracker row recorded `closing_commit_sha=ff9a6ce2f6db77c670adcf2874b3f86278dba4e8`. That SHA is the previously reviewed hx-registry-lookpath provenance-repair commit, not the review-bead lifecycle change itself. FEAT-019 explicitly says tracker-only backfills must omit `closing_commit_sha` instead of pointing replay at an unrelated metadata or earlier implementation commit, so the underlying close-path behavior still lets review/repair beads persist misleading replay provenance even after ff9a6ce repaired one specific bead row.
Suggested fix: Make `ddx bead close` omit `closing_commit_sha` when no new close commit is actually created or when the operation is metadata-only tracker maintenance, add regression coverage for that path, and repair hx-01c0012d so its closing provenance is absent rather than pointing at ff9a6ce.","id":"hx-ae141d75","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: omit misleading closing provenance for metadata-only review closes","updated_at":"2026-04-10T06:05:14.662412287Z"}

1452:{"acceptance":"A regression test models the production write order by placing the mirrored unkeyed session row before the routing outcome row and ddx agent usage still reports one session for that run; cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestAgentUsage -count=1 passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"ec667e0c023203bbcc6f78f1d96d53f55ea03bc2","created_at":"2026-04-10T13:52:22.213145531Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:208
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new cutoff-based dedupe only skips session rows whose timestamp is at or after the first routing outcome for a harness, but current runners write the session row before they append the routing outcome (cli/internal/agent/runner.go:169-170 and cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:162-163). For unkeyed runs without native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id, the mirrored session entry therefore remains just before the cutoff and is still counted, so ddx agent usage can continue double-counting the exact current-run duplicates this commit intended to remove. The new regression test encodes the opposite timestamp order (session after outcome), so it passes without exercising the real runtime path.
Suggested fix: Deduplicate unkeyed mirrored rows using a criterion that matches production write order, or emit a stable mirror identifier on every current run and key dedupe on that identifier; update the regression test so the session timestamp precedes the routing outcome timestamp as the runners do in production.","id":"hx-bd7f1344","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"parent":"ddx-f73cbe0d","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/agent_usage.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: unkeyed session dedupe misses real log write order","updated_at":"2026-04-10T16:16:31.711625046Z"}

1477:{"acceptance":"The execute-bead contract explicitly documents the supervisor-visible result fields and state enum, and API-001 only depends on that documented surface rather than inferred reason strings","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"759566de5f7a2a00d770290e9486c01ee0d50218","created_at":"2026-04-10T12:12:41.838488829Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:69
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The revised contract now says the supervisor captures the result state reported by `ddx agent execute-bead` and classifies outcomes such as execution failure, post-run check failure, land conflict, and success. The current command surface does not publish that enum anywhere: `ExecuteBeadResult` in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:18-36` exposes only `outcome` (`merged|preserved|no-changes|error`) plus a free-form `reason`, and FEAT-006 documents only landed/preserved iteration outcomes in the commit summary. A supervisor built from API-001 would have to reverse-engineer undocumented meanings from ad hoc strings, which is too brittle for a contract-level dependency.
Suggested fix: Add a documented, stable execute-bead result schema that includes the supervisor-facing state categories API-001 needs, or narrow API-001 so it only relies on the result fields that `execute-bead` actually guarantees today.","id":"hx-d021e212","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:api","area:agent","area:docs"],"parent":"ddx-315eddb0","priority":1,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md","status":"closed","title":"drift: define the execute-bead result surface API-001 expects","updated_at":"2026-04-10T16:18:32.493112053Z"}

File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:8
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 8eaf934b6d106b9f513fb76bb10f2091e67b87d5 improves ddx-83777abd by repointing its closing provenance at the actual implementation commit, but it stores only the 7-character prefix `d84aa2b` instead of the exact commit SHA. The tracker close contract documents `closing_commit_sha` as the exact closing commit SHA when known, and replay/audit use this field as durable provenance. The abbreviated prefix resolves today only because it is currently unique; as the repository grows it can become ambiguous or invalid, which would break later replay or audit runs for this bead.
Suggested fix: Rewrite ddx-83777abd.closing_commit_sha to the full 40-character commit SHA `d84aa2b457f234c07a8195346b09fd416f13d478`, and keep manual close/provenance normalization aligned on full object IDs for durable replay semantics.","id":"hx-dba4e969","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: store full closing_commit_sha for ddx-83777abd","updated_at":"2026-04-10T16:14:55.122318537Z"}

If install/doctor validation is implemented from SD-018 as written, built-in DDx skills will fail validation or require an undocumented breaking migration, which violates FEAT-018's backward-compatibility goal.

1514:{"acceptance":"The contract states an explicit queue-selection rule consistent with SD-004 ready ordering and HELIX eligibility ownership, and the generic execution validator is used only to validate structural executability rather than choose policy priority","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"759566de5f7a2a00d770290e9486c01ee0d50218","created_at":"2026-04-10T05:54:29.526101091Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:59
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: API-001 says the supervisor should read the ready bead set and choose the best candidate using the generic execution-ready validator. But the same contract says HELIX owns which beads are eligible for the loop, and SD-004 already defines deterministic ready ordering as priority then stable snapshot order. The generic DDx validator can say whether one bead is structurally executable; it does not define queue priority or workflow policy. Leaving candidate choice as 'best' via the validator either invents hidden DDx scheduling policy or makes the first worker's queue behavior undefined.
Suggested fix: Separate queue ordering from readiness validation. Specify whether the supervisor consumes a pre-ordered execution-ready queue (for example SD-004 ordering plus workflow eligibility filters) and then uses the validator only as a gate on the selected candidate.","id":"hx-ec50ff6b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:api","area:agent","area:bead","area:docs"],"parent":"ddx-315eddb0","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md","status":"closed","title":"drift: define supervisor queue selection independently from the generic validator","updated_at":"2026-04-10T16:18:32.447577504Z"}

1523:{"acceptance":"execute-bead rebases the worktree result onto current HEAD before attempting ff-merge; when the rebase succeeds the result lands without a merge commit; when the rebase conflicts the iteration is preserved under the hidden ref and the target branch remains unchanged; and tests cover the case where the target branch advanced during execution","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T02:28:56Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4071725","closing_commit_sha":"65134ca7caedd43b6dab6e567d001b36fb32b665","created_at":"2026-04-09T13:00:00Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","depends_on_id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","type":"blocks"}],"description":"FEAT-006 step 10 specifies 'land by rebase + fast-forward semantics' but the implementation only attempts `git merge --ff-only`.

## Current behavior
If the target branch has advanced since the base revision, the ff-merge fails and the result is preserved under a hidden ref instead of being rebased and landed.

## Fix required
Before attempting ff-merge, rebase the worktree commits onto the current branch HEAD. If the rebase succeeds, then ff-merge and keep linear history. If the rebase fails because of conflicts, preserve the iteration under the hidden ref, leave the target branch untouched, and report the non-landed result deterministically.","id":"hx-exec-bead-rebase-ff","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"Implement rebase + ff semantics for execute-bead land flow","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:31:23.760412906Z"}
1524:{"acceptance":"After execute-bead completes, dirty worktree changes are either restored via git stash pop or the user is explicitly warned with the stash ref so they can restore manually","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T19:15:50Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1385203","closing_commit_sha":"ada469a58561d9795471b0b02d10aa5c8a7f94aa","created_at":"2026-04-09T13:00:00Z","description":"execute-bead stashes dirty worktree changes but never restores them after execution.

## Current behavior
When execute-bead detects a dirty worktree, it calls git stash to checkpoint changes but never calls git stash pop. User's in-progress work remains silently stashed.

## Fix required
Add git stash pop (or git stash apply) in the defer cleanup after the worktree is removed, or document that stash is intentional and warn the user with the stash ref.","id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"Fix execute-bead dirty worktree stash/restore bug","updated_at":"2026-04-09T19:20:21.950105538Z"}

1531:{"acceptance":"API-001, FEAT-006, and the execute-bead command agree on a single owner for execution worktree creation and cleanup; the documented supervisor flow no longer requires nested or duplicated worktree management around one execute-bead attempt","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T12:38:53Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2540533","closing_commit_sha":"f577b91b349d66a9352bfd3d1332a5af82b3274f","created_at":"2026-04-10T12:12:41.773899952Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:67
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The updated contract still says the supervisor claims the bead, creates an isolated execution worktree, runs `ddx agent execute-bead` inside that worktree, and then removes the worktree. FEAT-006 defines `ddx agent execute-bead` itself as the workflow that creates the isolated worktree (step 4) and always removes it after preserve/land handling (step 12), and the shipped command implementation already does exactly that in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:261-274`. As written, the first shipped supervisor cannot follow API-001 without layering a second worktree lifecycle around one execute-bead attempt or introducing an undocumented lower-level mode. That leaves the core ownership boundary ambiguous and makes the contract non-executable for the intended wrapper design.
Suggested fix: Make one layer own the worktree lifecycle. Either change API-001 so the supervisor invokes `ddx agent execute-bead` from the project root and only reacts to its result, or introduce and document a lower-level execute-bead primitive that accepts an already-created worktree and update FEAT-006/API-001 together.","id":"hx-f9928f75","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high","area:api","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: avoid duplicating execute-bead worktree ownership in API-001","updated_at":"2026-04-10T12:45:39.684926309Z"}
1532:{"acceptance":"Given .ddx/plugins/<name> already exists as a real directory in the project, when ddx install <name> --local <path> installs a manifest whose root target is under ~/, then the command fails without --force and preserves the directory contents; with --force, it replaces the path with the compatibility symlink","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T09:07:04Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1935092","closing_commit_sha":"0bd4822376540bde7eb3f11712e23d56c6c50392","created_at":"2026-04-10T04:39:44.320637491Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/install.go:282
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: When a local plugin manifest sets install.root.target under ~/.ddx/plugins/<name>, installLocal now unconditionally removes .ddx/plugins/<name> in the current project before creating the compatibility symlink. That bypasses the command's existing force guard and silently deletes any pre-existing project-local plugin checkout or files even when --force was not provided.
Evidence: Reproduced by creating an existing worktree directory at .ddx/plugins/sample-plugin with a sentinel file, then running ddx install sample-plugin --local <path> for a manifest rooted at ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin. The command returned success, sentinel_exists=false, and the directory was replaced by a symlink.

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:403
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit c38d7bbb2cf47baed8197c27cac19cb4adcd8644 documents ddx bead update <id> --set closing_commit_sha=<sha> as the supported provenance-repair path and adds a happy-path test for closed beads, but the underlying bead update implementation still routes closing_commit_sha through the generic --set handler without any normalization or verification. Unlike bead close --commit, which resolves the value through git rev-parse --verify <sha>^{commit}, bead update accepts arbitrary strings such as not-a-sha and persists them to closed beads. That leaves FEAT-019 replay and audit provenance one bad repair away from silently storing an invalid or ambiguous commit boundary even though the docs now present this as the safe replacement for hand-editing storage.
Suggested fix: Teach ddx bead update --set closing_commit_sha=... to use the same commit-resolution path as bead close --commit and reject invalid or non-commit values, then add a regression test proving invalid repair SHAs fail and valid abbreviated SHAs are normalized to full commits.","id":"hx-fd99632b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:bead"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: validate closing_commit_sha repairs before persisting","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:02:53.028428892Z"}

1540:{"acceptance":"Registry.Discover() uses the same LookPath abstraction as Runner.resolveHarness; a test with injected LookPath controls both routing and discovery results","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T01:34:29Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3930774","closing_commit_sha":"fb667a6c6ecb1e6dd8a44c65964dcb9d474aff41","created_at":"2026-04-09T13:00:00Z","description":"<context-digest>
Review area: agent harness discovery. Evidence covers Registry.Discover LookPath injection alignment with Runner.
</context-digest>
Registry.Discover() calls exec.LookPath directly instead of using the Runner's injected LookPath function.

## Impact
Tests that inject a custom LookPath cannot fully control Discover() behavior. Any future LookPath customization (caching, PATH filtering) won't apply to discovery.

## Fix required
Add a LookPath field to Registry (or pass it via constructor) so Discover() uses the same abstraction as Runner.","id":"hx-registry-lookpath","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Wire LookPath injection through Registry.Discover","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:39:04.66086581Z"}
